payload.title: Spike: .trs/.trust file extension for editor identity + activation-by-extension
payload.description: Time-boxed spike, NOT a commitment. Appeal: an extension makes a file unambiguously strict (clean LSP/highlighter trigger, no in-file marker). Hard part: it fights cargo's build model — cargo hard-codes src/main.rs|lib.rs and resolves `mod foo;` to foo.rs, so a new extension means either transpiling .trs -> a shadow .rs tree before cargo runs (a real build step to maintain) or a wrapper that still must exist to intercept. Conclusion to validate: an extension buys ergonomics but does NOT remove tooling — it only moves where lowering triggers. Deliverable: a short writeup with a recommendation (likely: defer until cargo-subcommand flow lands, then revisit as a nicety). Do NOT start before the subcmd + config stories.
